description: This integrated module bridges macro-level state administration with micro-level decentralized governance. SSC CGL frequently tests constitutional provisions, executive vs. legislative powers, bicameral nuances, and the evolutionary milestones of local self-government in India.

learningFlow.focus: Constitutional structure under Part VI of the Indian Constitution (Articles 152–237).
Appointment, executive powers, judicial immunity, discretionary powers, and ordinance-making power (Art. 213).
Real executive head, appointment by Governor, relations with Council of Ministers, and role in State Planning.
Collective responsibility (Art. 164), individual responsibility, advice binding nature, and Cabinet vs. Council dynamics.
Unicameral vs. Bicameral setups, composition, tenure, and constitutional provisions (Art. 168–212).
Direct elections, maximum/minimum strength, Speaker/Deputy Speaker powers, and dissolution rules.
Abolition & Creation (Art. 169), composition breakdown (1/3rd, 1/12th, etc.), and limited legislative powers.
Money Bills vs. Ordinary Bills, Governor's assent, reservation of bills for President (Art. 200/201), and budgetary process.
Evolutionary committees (Balwant Rai Mehta, Ashok Mehta, LM Singhvi) and the philosophy of democratic decentralization.
Insertion of Part IX and 11th Schedule (29 functional items); mandatory vs. voluntary provisions.
Three-tier system (Gram, Intermediate, District level), Gram Sabha, reservation for women, and State Finance/Election Commissions.
Insertion of Part IX-A and 12th Schedule (18 functional items); structural framework for urban governance.
Nagar Panchayats, Municipal Councils, Municipal Corporations, Ward Committees, and District Planning Committees.
Comprehensive consolidated memory mapping of high-frequency articles from Art. 153 to Art. 243ZG.
